linting.notes: Main lint command is 'make lint' for Go code. Full pre-commit checks run via 'make precommit' which includes go mod tidy, golangci-lint, and copyright header fixes.

testing.test_directory: pkg/ (unit tests), tests/ (e2e/integration tests), services/uds_tokenizer/tests/ (Python tests)
testing.mock_patterns: Uses miniredis for Redis mocking, testcontainers for UDS tokenizer service
testing.notes: Two test modes: 'make unit-test-uds' (Go-only, no Python CGo dependencies) and 'make unit-test-embedded' (requires Python 3.12 + vLLM). E2E tests require Docker/Podman for testcontainers. Python tests run via 'make uds-tokenizer-service-test' using pytest.

ci.notes: CI runs on ubuntu-latest with Go version extracted from go.mod. Requires system dependencies: libzmq3-dev, pkg-config, python3.12, python3.12-dev, python3.12-venv, clang-format. Python 3.12 is added via ppa:deadsnakes/ppa. All checks in ci-pr-checks.yaml are gating for pull requests to main/dev branches.

build.build_command: make build (builds both UDS-only and embedded binaries)
build.notes: Two build modes: 'make build-uds' (Go-only, no Python) and 'make build-embedded' (with Python embedded tokenizers). Embedded mode requires Python 3.12, vLLM 0.14.0, and CGo setup. ZMQ library is auto-installed via 'make download-zmq'.

conventions.test_naming: TestFunctionName for functions, TestSuiteName for test suites (using testify)
conventions.import_style: Standard Go import grouping (stdlib, external, internal)
conventions.notes: Tests use testify/suite and testify/assert. Some tests use Ginkgo/Gomega framework. Go code follows golangci-lint rules with 130 char line limit.

setup_commands: apt-get update && apt-get install -y libzmq3-dev pkg-config python3-dev python3-venv clang-format git make

test_commands.notes: All unit tests passed (47+ test cases)
Direct go test command works, all packages passed

container_recipe.notes: Container uses Python 3.13 instead of 3.12, but tests pass. For embedded tokenizers, need CGO_ENABLED=1 and Python config flags. E2E tests require Docker-in-Docker or Podman socket access for testcontainers.

validation.summary: install OK, build OK, lint OK (0 issues), unit tests OK (47+ tests passed in ~20s)
